stemHtml: After losing all engines and starting a glide descent, the pilot lowers the flaps in an attempt to increase the glide range. The pilot acted...

options.a: Correctly, because lowering the flaps can help improve the glide range and potentially reach a farther landing site.
options.b: Correctly, as lowering the flaps can help the pilot achieve a more efficient glide and extend the range for landing options.
options.c: Incorrectly, because lowering the flaps decreases glide range and increases glide angle.
options.d: Incorrectly, because lowering the flaps decreases glide range and decreases glide angle.
